1.texto_original: L’ANNO diciassettesimo di Peca, figliuolo di Remalia, Achaz, figliuolo di Iotam, re di Giuda, cominciò a regnare.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Re 16:1

"L’ANNO diciassettesimo di Peca, figliuolo di Remalia, Achaz, figliuolo di Iotam, re di Giuda, cominciò a regnare."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 2 Re 16:1

Philological Analysis

"L’ANNO diciassettesimo di Peca, figliuolo di Remalia, Achaz, figliuolo di Iotam, re di Giuda, cominciò a regnare."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"L’ANNO diciassettesimo di Peca, figliuolo di Remalia, Achaz, figliuolo di Iotam, re di Giuda, cominciò a regnare."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

14.texto_original: E fece levar via l’Altar di rame, ch’era davanti al Signore, d’innanzi alla Casa, acciocchè non fosse fra il suo altare, e la Casa del Signore; e lo mise allato a quell’altro altare, verso il Settentrione.
14.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Re 16:14

"E fece levar via l’Altar di rame, ch’era davanti al Signore, d’innanzi alla Casa, acciocchè non fosse fra il suo altare, e la Casa del Signore; e lo mise allato a quell’altro altare, verso il Settentrione."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
14.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 2 Re 16:14

Philological Analysis

"E fece levar via l’Altar di rame, ch’era davanti al Signore, d’innanzi alla Casa, acciocchè non fosse fra il suo altare, e la Casa del Signore; e lo mise allato a quell’altro altare, verso il Settentrione."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
14.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"E fece levar via l’Altar di rame, ch’era davanti al Signore, d’innanzi alla Casa, acciocchè non fosse fra il suo altare, e la Casa del Signore; e lo mise allato a quell’altro altare, verso il Settentrione."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

15.texto_original: E il re Achaz comandò al sacerdote Uria, dicendo: Ardi sopra l’altar grande l’olocausto della mattina, e l’offerta della sera, e l’olocausto del re, e la sua offerta; e gli olocausti di tutto il popolo del paese, con le loro offerte di panatica, e da spandere; e spandi sopra esso tutto il sangue degli olocausti, e tutto il sangue de’ sacrificii; ma quant’è all’Altar di rame, a me starà il ricercarlo.

17.texto_original: Il re Achaz, oltre a ciò, tagliò a pezzi i basamenti fatti a quadri, e levò le conche d’in su que’ basamenti; mise anche giù il mare d’in su i buoi di rame, che erano sotto di esso, e lo posò sopra il pavimento di pietra.
